---
title: "Object Extraction with Image Thresholding"
description: "Using binary thresholding and Otsu's method to extract objects from image backgrounds."
date: "2026-07-22"
categories:
  - Computer Vision
  - Python
  - OpenCV
execute: false
---

## Project overview

This project explores how image thresholding can be used to separate an object from its background.

The implementation compares manual binary thresholding with Otsu's automatic threshold selection method.

## The problem

Add a description of the image-processing problem here.

## Project objective

The objective is to generate a binary mask that identifies the foreground object and use that mask to extract it from the original image.

## Input images

The project uses images of a mango and a cup as initial examples.

## Technical approach

The processing workflow includes:

1. Loading the original image.
2. Converting the image to grayscale.
3. Applying binary thresholding.
4. Applying Otsu's thresholding method.
5. Generating a binary mask.
6. Extracting the foreground object.
7. Saving the resulting images.

## Results

Add the resulting images and explain the differences between the thresholding approaches.

## Repository

[View the project repository](https://github.com/mateocordobatoro/machine-learning-ii)
